projectDescription: A low-cost proof-of-concept guided rocket and launcher prototype using ESP32 microcontrollers, 3D-printed components, and PD-controlled canard fins for roll stabilization.
keyFeatures: ESP32-based flight computer with MPU6050 IMU for roll stabilization
PD controller driving four canard servos (±12° max deflection)
Folding fin design for compact launcher integration
WiFi SoftAP ground station relaying telemetry via UDP
Python Tkinter dashboard with live telemetry plots and PID tuning
GPS, compass (QMC5883L), and barometer (BMP180) sensor fusion on launcher
Digital and physical launch triggering with arm/safe state machine
Heartbeat watchdog and abort sequences for safety
publicApiSurface: Serial2 command: ARM — arms the rocket flight computer
Serial2 command: IGNITE — triggers motor ignition servo
Serial2 command: CALIBRATE — re-zeroes gyro offset
Serial2 command: PID,<Kp>,<Kd> — updates stabilization gains at runtime
UDP command: launch — digital launch trigger from dashboard
UDP command: calibrate — remote gyro calibration
UDP command: PID,<Kp>,<Kd> — PID tuning from dashboard
UDP telemetry T,<ms>,<roll>,<rate>,<output> — rocket roll telemetry
UDP telemetry STATUS:<state>,<Kp>,<Kd>,<skew> — rocket state broadcast
UDP telemetry ENV,<lat>,<lon>,<alt>,<gpsState> — launcher environment data
calibrateGyro() — averages 200 gyro samples to compute bias offset
Launcher state machine: SAFE → ARMING → READY → IGNITING
